draftNote: 2 of five queued off a bottom-two lane: Mr_Meat31 (midlane→offlane), Cuban Noobster (jungle→carry).

coaching.headline: A 22-minute loss at 8 kills to 19 deaths, 0–5 towers, majors 10–14 against — the fights were picked deep in their half without the numbers.
coaching.team: All three game-defining fights were contested on their side of the map and lost — 0–2 at their Fangtooth at 9.7, 2–4 there again at 16.7, 0–3 at their Orb Prime at 21.9, a combined 2–9 — and they cashed those into Fangtooth at 10 and 17 and Orb Prime at 22. The macro reads say why: Kira was alive and winning her lane but never rotated (9.7 and 21.9), and Crunch was pinned losing to Countess, which made those the wrong fights to start. The enemy ran only 1/5 on their meta builds — the win was there. Countess did 18k magical and just 2/5 of you itemized magic resist.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: Contesting their Fangtooth and Prime without the bodies — either shove the winning lanes and rotate them in first, or concede the prize and take farm and towers; 2–9 across those three fights was the whole game.

perPlayer.7c8ab99c-31c5-4ea5-a6fa-e0d6417c3f1b: Day-one Ikra mid (1/4/0) — there's no build or matchup data on her yet, which is exactly when to skip deep coin-flips: the 16.7 Fangtooth and 21.9 Prime fights you joined went 2–4 and 0–3, and your death 31 seconds before the 20-minute fight left it a 4v5.
perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Kira won the lane all game, but the macro read flags it twice: at 9.7 and 21.9 you were alive and ahead while the team lost 0–2 and 0–3 across the map — shove and rotate, that's the numbers advantage. Damage at 568/min against your 938 norm says the lane lead never got spent.
perPlayer.a0c393cc-1437-4bdc-8168-64676ce2166c: Serath 3/5/2 in all three lost Fangtooth/Prime fights with no magic resist against a 3-magical comp — into Countess's 18k that's fighting without an answer. Damage ran 450/min against your 675 norm; when the deep contest isn't set up, reset and take camps instead.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Crunch was pinned by Countess all game (0/4/0) — the fights the team started while you were stuck weren't on you. But at 521 mitigated/min against your 997 norm with your usual core (Augmentation, Mutilator, Draconum) never built, you also never got tanky enough to change one.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Phase won the support lane all game, but the 20-minute read applies to you: alive and ahead while the team fought a 4v5 across the map — shove the advantage and rotate before the team commits. Four deaths in 22 minutes says the commits came anyway.

threats: Chain-CC risk (1 hard-CC abilities, ~0.8s of lockdown): Countess Feast (suppress 0.75s). Don't group tight into it — stagger the engage and hold mobility/cleanse for the key one.
Heal-stacking: Zinx. Anti-heal is mandatory against this comp — you brought none.
Dive threat on your backline: Countess. Assign peel for your carry and ward your flanks.
synergy: Your engage/CC: Crunch Right Crunch (knockup 1.25s). Your kills come from landing burst INSIDE that CC window — whoever lands it, the rest must follow up immediately.
You have sustain (Phase) — favor the longer fight where your heals out-grind them.
Strong AoE/teamfight kit — group at choke points and fight on top of objectives.

ourKits.combo: Mercy poke → stack Vengeance → Shadow Glide reposition → Purge ult
ourKits.winCondition: stack Vengeance and shred with the channeled Purge
ourKits.playWith: peel so she can channel Purge safely
ourKits.playAgainst: interrupt/CC her during Purge; punish her short dash cooldown

ourKits.combo: Ascend dive → Heaven's Fury multi-strike → Chastise slow → Heresy form ult, resets on takedowns
ourKits.winCondition: dive the backline and snowball Heresy resets
ourKits.playWith: a frontline that creates her dive angle
ourKits.playAgainst: peel the backline — deny the first kill and her resets dry up

ourKits.combo: Forward Crunch gap-close stun → Right Crunch knockup → empowered Left Crunch, sustain via Re-Crunch
ourKits.winCondition: all-in a squishy with the empowered combo and heal back through Re-Crunch
ourKits.passive: post-ability empowered basic that refunds cooldowns
ourKits.playWith: peel-stripping engage so he reaches the backline
ourKits.playAgainst: peel/CC him off — without resets he's stranded; anti-heal blunts Re-Crunch

ourKits.combo: Telekinetic Link an ally → Psychic Flare blind → Energy Lance → Hyperflux buff ult
ourKits.winCondition: buff a hyper-carry through the Link and Hyperflux for a teamfight
ourKits.passive: abilities heal her and her linked ally
ourKits.playWith: the strongest carry to Link and supercharge
ourKits.playAgainst: kill the linked carry, not Phase; break the link timing

enemyKits.combo: Shadow Slip in → Eventide → Blade Siphon → Feast suppress ult, Haemorrhage true-damage
enemyKits.winCondition: Feast-suppress a squishy and delete them with true-damage burst
enemyKits.passive: two-ability hits apply a %max-HP true-damage bleed
enemyKits.playWith: a team to clean up after her pick
enemyKits.playAgainst: Feast is her whole engage — bait it / peel; she's fragile, punish the recast window

enemyKits.combo: Ricochet stun → Bad Medicine zone → Infuse heal an ally → Neural Regenesis revive
enemyKits.winCondition: keep an ally alive with Infuse + the revive ult and poke with Ricochet
enemyKits.playWith: a carry she can pocket-heal and revive
enemyKits.playAgainst: she can't heal herself — dive HER; bait the revive before committing

enemyKits.combo: Pounce → Rumble slow → Boulder Throw → Behemoth ult to brawl
enemyKits.winCondition: engage with Pounce, grow with Behemoth and out-sustain the fight
enemyKits.passive: ramping health regen for jungle sustain
enemyKits.playWith: follow-up damage while he front-lines and CCs
enemyKits.playAgainst: anti-heal and kiting beat his sustain brawl; burst him before Behemoth

enemyKits.combo: stance-swap with Zero Shift → Tachyon Lance (ranged) or melee chain → Limit Break on Overheat
enemyKits.winCondition: build Heat and burst with Limit Break, flexing range and melee
enemyKits.passive: damage builds Heat → Overheat unlocks Limit Break
enemyKits.playWith: peel that lets her ramp Heat safely
enemyKits.playAgainst: pressure her before Overheat; CC during the melee stance

enemyKits.combo: Soulfire waves → Mortal Coil pull-zone → Stupor orb → Abyssal Rift zone
enemyKits.winCondition: zone a fight with Mortal Coil + ult and chunk from range
enemyKits.passive: consumes essences from kills for power
enemyKits.playWith: frontline to hold enemies in her zones
enemyKits.playAgainst: fragile and reliant on her zones — flank/dive around the telegraphs
